The file `3rdparty/packages/basic09/basic09.asm` was annotated on 2026-05-15 using /annotate-asm.

**Why:** The file was a disassembly of the BASIC09 interpreter for OS-9/NitrOS-9, with generic u-prefix (data) and L-prefix (code) labels generated by a disassembler. The goal was to replace them with meaningful names.

**What was done:**
- All 184 `u`-prefix DP-relative data variable labels renamed (e.g., u0000â†’DpBase, u002Eâ†’StdoutPath, u007Dâ†’TmpBufCount)
- All 1073 `L`-prefix code labels renamed (e.g., L0DBBâ†’ResetTmpBuf, L3CB5â†’VarCopyBase, L323Fâ†’StmtJmpBase)
- Six real-arithmetic include files also updated: basic09.real.add.{63,68}.asm, basic09.real.mul.{63,68}.asm, basic09.real.div.{63,68}.asm
- Annotation header added to the banner comment block
- Binary output verified IDENTICAL to pre-annotation baseline (CRC $41051B, module size $5A39)

**How to apply:** When working on basic09.asm, the labels are now human-readable. The assembly command is:
```
lwasm --6309 --format=os9 --pragma=pcaspcr,nosymbolcase,condundefzero,undefextern,dollarnotlocal,noforwardrefmax --includedir=./defs --includedir=./3rdparty/packages/basic09 -o basic09_6309 ./3rdparty/packages/basic09/basic09.asm
```
